As told earlier, a yama is 3% Khati (1 hr 30 min= 90 min)
time. This time span is not divided equally among tatvas.
How much span should be ascribed to each tatva is described
below-

40 Secrets of Birth Time Rectification
~ ·::nfr rm:Fn 'fT fllfkc;i·
flC!l flTi:N 'JfPir:~"~J!<llfidiMJC'I_ 1· I
-~·~·
[114 Khati, I Khati, % Khati, Y2 Khati, 1,4 Khati is the time
span ascribed to each tatva]
That is - Earth = 30 min; Water= 24 min; Fire= 18 min;
Air = 12 min; Sky = 6 min. One more sloka with the same
meaning is given below-
ar.t#f fflrlK fT ?lll1if IJ1~4CJ>cb

(meaning is the same as told above)
There is a sloka in Dasadhyayi commentary ofBrihatjataka
that describes all the above details together in a comprehensive
way.
